callout: You answered '10' for the value of the digit 0 in 701. The correct answer is 0.
explanation: The value of a digit is digit × place value. The digit 0 in 701 is in the tens place, so its value is 0 × 10 = 0. Zero multiplied by anything is always zero — no matter which column it is in, a zero digit contributes nothing to the number's total value. You found the right column (tens = 10), but the digit itself is 0, so the value is 0.

questions.text: In 507, what is the value of the digit 0?
questions.working: 0 is in the tens place. Value = 0 × 10 = 0. Zero in any position is always worth 0.

questions.text: In 302, what is the value of the digit 0?
questions.working: 0 is in the tens place. Value = 0 × 10 = 0.

questions.text: In 4035, what is the value of the digit 0?
questions.working: 0 is in the hundreds place. Value = 0 × 100 = 0. Even in hundreds, a zero digit adds nothing.

questions.text: In 346, what is the value of the digit 4?
questions.working: 4 is in the tens place. Value = 4 × 10 = 40. Compare: if it were 0 in the tens place, 0 × 10 = 0.

questions.text: In 3070, what is the value of the digit 0 in the hundreds place?
questions.working: The 0 in the hundreds place: value = 0 × 100 = 0. The digit 7 is in the tens place (value = 70) — but the digit 0 is always 0 regardless of position.
